---
title: "Run a turnkey pipeline"
description: "End-to-end composed pipelines, and reference-atlas generation."
categories: [openpipeline_composed]
order: 70
---

[`openpipeline_composed`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_composed}<span class="swatch openpipeline_composed"></span>

The **`openpipeline_composed`** package bundles the individual stages into
turnkey, end-to-end pipelines. Instead of running
[ingestion](ingest-10x.qmd) → [processing](process-samples.qmd) →
[integration](integrate.qmd) → [annotation](annotate.qmd) as separate commands,
one composed workflow chains them together.

## The turnkey pipelines

| Workflow | What it does |
|---|---|
| [`single_cell/process_integrate_annotate`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/process_integrate_annotate.qmd) | The flagship: process → integrate → annotate in one run. Pick the integration method (Harmony, scVI, Scanorama, BBKNN) and annotation method (CellTypist, harmony_knn, scANVI, scvi_knn, SingleR). |
| [`single_cell/parallel_integration`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_integration.qmd) | Run several integration methods on the same cohort and compare them. |
| [`single_cell/parallel_annotation`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_annotation.qmd) | Run several annotation methods and combine them by **consensus vote**, more robust than any single method. |
| [`single_cell/parallel_subtyping`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_subtyping.qmd) | Resolve fine-grained cell states (subtypes) within annotated clusters. |

## Build a reference atlas

::: {.callout-important}
Reference-atlas generation is the headline use case for these pipelines. Running
`process_integrate_annotate` across a large, multi-donor cohort produces **one
integrated, annotated object** that every later run can consume as the reference
for [label transfer](annotate.qmd), the annotation methods (`harmony_knn`,
`scvi_knn`, scANVI/scArches) all map new query cells onto exactly this kind of
atlas.
:::

Build the atlas once, reuse it everywhere:

- **Integrate the whole cohort** so donor and batch effects are corrected across
  every sample that will define the reference.
- **Annotate it**, ideally combining methods with `parallel_annotation`'s
  consensus vote for labels you can trust as ground truth.
- **Refine cell states** with `parallel_subtyping` to resolve fine-grained
  populations before publishing.

The resulting atlas becomes the fixed reference new datasets are annotated
against, so every downstream analysis speaks the same set of cell-type labels.

## Example: process_integrate_annotate

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline_composed.git \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/single_cell/process_integrate_annotate/main.nf \
  --param_list samples.csv \
  --publish_dir results/
```

:::{.callout-note}
`openpipeline_composed` pins core `openpipeline` plus `openpipeline_qc` and
biobox. Check the [reference](../reference/index.qmd) (filter by
`openpipeline_composed`) for the exact argument list, and its Viash Hub page for
run instructions.
:::
